source_paper_title: A Stochastic Sequential Quadratic Optimization Algorithm for Nonlinear-Equality-Constrained Optimization with Rank-Deficient Jacobians
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Berahas_2024, title={A Stochastic Sequential Quadratic Optimization Algorithm for Nonlinear-Equality-Constrained Optimization with Rank-Deficient Jacobians}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2021.0154}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2021.0154}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Berahas, Albert S. and Curtis, Frank E. and O’Neill, Michael J. and Robinson, Daniel P.}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2212–2248} }
context:
Section 4.4 (Complementary Events), page 25.

However, it remains an open question whether it is possible to obtain a similar guarantee if/when a subsequence of stochastic gradient estimates diverges.
title: Convergence guarantees when stochastic gradient estimates diverge in vanishing-merit regime
background_latex:
Consider the stochastic equality-constrained optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in \mathbb{R}^n} f(x) \quad \text{s.t.}\quad c(x)=0,
\]
where \(f(x)=\mathbb{E}[F(x,\iota)]\) for a random variable \(\iota\) and smooth functions \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(c:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^m\). Let \(J(x):=\nabla c(x)^T\in\mathbb{R}^{m\times n}\) be the constraint Jacobian.

Define the constraint-violation measure
\[
\varphi(x):=\|c(x)\|_2.
\]
(Equivalently, one may consider \(\|c(x)\|_2^2\); stationarity conditions scale accordingly.) A point \(x\) is stationary for \(\varphi\) if either \(c(x)=0\) or \(c(x)\neq 0\) and
\[
\nabla \varphi(x)= J(x)^T\frac{c(x)}{\|c(x)\|_2}=0.
\]

An iterative stochastic SQP-type method generates iterates \(\{x_k\}_{k\ge 0}\) and uses stochastic gradient estimates \(g_k\) for \(\nabla f(x_k)\), e.g., unbiased with finite second moment,
\[
\mathbb{E}_k[g_k]=\nabla f(x_k),\qquad \mathbb{E}_k[\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2^2]\le M,
\]
where \(\mathbb{E}_k[\cdot]\) is conditional expectation given the algorithm has reached \(x_k\).

The method employs an exact-penalty merit function
\[
\phi(x,\tau)= \tau f(x)+\|c(x)\|_2,
\]
with an adaptively updated merit parameter \(\tau_k\ge 0\). One regime of interest is when \(\tau_k\to 0\) (the algorithm places vanishing weight on the objective term). In analyses where \(\tau_k\to 0\), a key additional assumption that enables convergence is that the stochastic gradient errors remain uniformly bounded along the run, i.e., \(\sup_k \|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2<\infty\).

Define the problematic event
\[
E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}:=\{\tau_k\to 0\ \text{and}\ \sup_{k\ge 0}\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2=\infty\},
\]
i.e., the merit parameter vanishes while a subsequence of stochastic gradient estimates diverges away from the true gradients.
problem_definition_latex:
Develop convergence guarantees for stochastic SQP-type methods for equality-constrained problems in the event
\[
E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}:=\{\tau_k\to 0\ \text{and}\ \sup_{k\ge 0}\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2=\infty\}.
\]
In particular, establish conditions under which one can still guarantee that the iterates satisfy an asymptotic stationarity property for the constraint-violation measure \(\varphi(x)=\|c(x)\|_2\) (e.g., \(\liminf_{k\to\infty}\|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|_2=0\), or another appropriate stationarity measure), despite the divergence of the stochastic gradient estimation errors while \(\tau_k\to 0\).
latex:

Develop convergence guarantees for stochastic SQP-type methods for equality-constrained problems in the event
\[
E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}:=\{\tau_k\to 0\ \text{and}\ \sup_{k\ge 0}\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2=\infty\}.

open_problem_quote: However, it remains an open question whether it is possible to obtain a similar guarantee if/when a subsequence of stochastic gradient estimates diverges.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for a positive convergence guarantee under an ill-behaved noise regime, not a sharp universal inequality that can be refuted by a finite-dimensional instance search. While one could search for pathological examples, the space of algorithms/noise processes is too unconstrained for an automated finite counterexample hunt to be decisive.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can programmatically explore toy problem families, noise models that induce divergent gradient estimates, and modified update rules for \(\tau_k\) or stepsizes, with a computable fitness signal based on observed constraint-stationarity measures. However, translating empirical behavior into general guarantees is indirect and would likely require additional human theoretical insight.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Berahas--Curtis--O'Neill--Robinson, 2023) develops a stochastic SQP method for equality constraints with potentially rank-deficient Jacobians, using a Byrd--Omojokun-style normal/tangential decomposition and an exact-penalty merit \(\phi(x,\tau)=\tau f(x)+\|c(x)\|_2\). Their convergence analysis splits into regimes depending on the behavior of the adaptive merit parameter \(\tau_k\). When \(\tau_k\) stabilizes at a sufficiently small positive value, they obtain expected decrease bounds for a model reduction \(\Delta \ell\) and derive asymptotic stationarity measures involving \(\|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|\) and a reduced-gradient term \(\|Z_k^T\nabla f(x_k)\|\). When \(\tau_k\to 0\), they show the method transitions toward minimizing constraint violation \(\varphi(x)=\|c(x)\|\), proving \(\liminf_k \|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|=0\) under an additional deterministic boundedness assumption on the stochastic gradient errors.

The open difficulty is precisely the complementary event \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\): \(\tau_k\to 0\) while \(\sup_k\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|=\infty\). In this regime, the merit update (which compares predicted constraint reduction against a noisy objective model term) can be repeatedly perturbed by rare but arbitrarily large gradient-estimation errors, undermining the key inequalities used to bound constraint decrease (e.g., the bounds controlling terms like \(\alpha_k\tau_k(\nabla f(x_k)-g_k)^T d_k\)). Existing related work either assumes LICQ and/or enforces accuracy control (e.g., Na--Anitescu--Kolar, 2021) or provides deterministic rank-deficient foundations (Curtis--Nocedal--W{"a}chter, 2009) that do not address unbounded stochastic errors.

At present, there is no forward-citation evidence (none provided) of a paper resolving \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\). Promising directions suggested by the structure of the source analysis include: (i) replacing the deterministic boundedness requirement with high-probability or almost-sure control via robustification (e.g., gradient clipping or median-of-means estimators) so that the effective noise entering the merit update is uniformly bounded; (ii) modifying the merit-parameter update to depend on truncated/filtered objective-model terms so that large \(\|g_k\|\) events cannot dominate when \(\tau_k\) is small; and (iii) developing a supermartingale argument directly for \(\varphi(x_k)\) that treats the objective-related stochastic terms as summable perturbations when \(\tau_k\to 0\), possibly under tail assumptions (sub-exponential/sub-Weibull) rather than uniform boundedness. These directions would aim to recover an asymptotic feasibility-stationarity guarantee such as \(\liminf_{k\to\infty}\|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|_2=0\) even when gradient errors are unbounded but sufficiently rare or sufficiently integrable.

sources.title: A Stochastic Sequential Quadratic Optimization Algorithm for Nonlinear Equality Constrained Optimization with Rank-Deficient Jacobians
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces a stochastic SQP method with normal/tangential step decomposition and an exact-penalty merit \(\phi(x,\tau)=\tau f(x)+\|c(x)\|_2\), allowing rank-deficient Jacobians. Proves convergence in expectation under several regimes: (i) constant sufficiently small \(\tau_k\) leading to primal-dual-type stationarity measures; (ii) vanishing \(\tau_k\to 0\) with an added deterministic boundedness assumption on gradient errors, yielding \(\liminf_k\|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|_2=0\). Explicitly identifies the open event \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\) where \(\tau_k\to 0\) and stochastic gradient errors diverge.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: extend the vanishing-merit analysis to the case where \(\tau_k\to 0\) but \(\sup_k\|g_k-\nabla f(x_k)\|_2=\infty\). It provides the algorithmic structure, notation, and the existing convergence proof that breaks without bounded gradient errors.

sources.title: Sequential Quadratic Optimization for Nonlinear Equality Constrained Stochastic Optimization
sources.bibtex:
@article{BerahasCurtisRobinsonZhou2021StochasticSQP,
  title   = {Sequential Quadratic Optimization for Nonlinear Equality Constrained Stochastic Optimization},
  author  = {Berahas, Albert S. and Curtis, Frank E. and Robinson, Daniel P. and Zhou, Baoyu},
  journal = {SIAM Journal on Optimization},
  volume  = {31},
  number  = {2},
  pages   = {1352--1379},
  year    = {2021},
  doi     = {10.1137/20M1342903},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1137/20M1342903}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops a stochastic SQP framework for equality-constrained stochastic optimization under LICQ (constraint Jacobians uniformly full row rank). Establishes expected convergence to stationarity measures for the constrained problem using an exact-penalty merit and step decomposition, but does not address rank-deficient Jacobians or the \(\tau_k\to 0\) regime with unbounded gradient noise.
sources.connection: Provides the predecessor analysis techniques (merit parameter updates, model decrease, expected descent) that the 2023 paper generalizes. It does not resolve the open event \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\) but is key background for how bounded-variance noise is handled when \(\tau_k\) stays bounded away from 0.

sources.title: A matrix-free algorithm for equality constrained optimization problems with rank deficient Jacobians
sources.bibtex:
@article{CurtisNocedalWachter2009MatrixFreeRankDeficient,
  title   = {A matrix-free algorithm for equality constrained optimization problems with rank deficient Jacobians},
  author  = {Curtis, Frank E. and Nocedal, Jorge and W{"a}chter, Andreas},
  journal = {SIAM Journal on Optimization},
  volume  = {20},
  number  = {3},
  pages   = {1224--1249},
  year    = {2009},
  doi     = {10.1137/080724863},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.1137/080724863}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Presents a deterministic matrix-free SQP/trust-region framework for equality-constrained optimization that remains well-defined with rank-deficient Jacobians, using normal/tangential decomposition and Krylov methods. Proves global convergence to feasibility-stationarity measures such as \(\|J(x_k)^T c(x_k)\|\to 0\) under appropriate conditions.
sources.connection: Supplies deterministic rank-deficient Jacobian techniques (normal step Cauchy decrease, tangential step in \(\mathrm{Null}(J_k)\)) that underpin the stochastic algorithm. However, it does not treat stochastic gradients or vanishing-merit/noise-divergence interactions.

sources.title: An adaptive stochastic sequential quadratic programming with differentiable exact augmented Lagrangians
sources.theorem_or_result: Proposes an adaptive stochastic SQP method using differentiable exact augmented Lagrangians and a line-search-like mechanism that may require increasingly accurate stochastic gradients. Assumes LICQ at iterates and focuses on convergence under controlled stochasticity.
sources.connection: Represents an alternative stochastic SQP line that avoids vanishing-merit exact-penalty behavior by using augmented Lagrangians and accuracy control. It does not address rank deficiency nor the regime \(\tau_k\to 0\) with diverging gradient errors, but suggests that controlling gradient accuracy is one way to preclude \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\).

sources.title: Stochastic subgradient method converges on tame functions
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes convergence of the stochastic subgradient method for broad classes of nonsmooth nonconvex (tame/definable) objectives under standard step-size and noise assumptions, using tools such as supermartingale arguments and stability properties.
sources.connection: Provides general stochastic-approximation machinery that might be adapted to analyze constraint-violation minimization \(\varphi(x)=\|c(x)\|\) when the algorithm effectively behaves like a stochastic method on \(\varphi\). However, it does not directly handle the coupled merit-parameter update and unbounded gradient noise in \(E_{\tau,\mathrm{zero,bad}}\).
